# Eightfold

Eightfold is shipping agent-driven end-to-end evaluation and workforce analytics. The "Infinite Workforce" capabilities (360 Interviews, Workforce Readiness) moved to general availability in July 2026, collapsing screening, functional, and technical assessment into a single agent-orchestrated interview and providing a board-ready dashboard of how much of each role AI can do versus what the workforce actually captures. For enterprise TA teams, Eightfold is positioning agentic evaluation and workforce planning as core platform features, not point solutions. The risk: agent-run interviews are already live in production; pressure-test auditability and adverse impact before letting agents gate candidates.

## Timeline

- **15 Jul 2026** — Eightfold moved its "Infinite Workforce" capabilities to general availability, including 360 Interviews — which collapses screening, functional, and technical assessment into a single agent-orchestrated interview — and Workforce Readiness, a board-ready dashboard scoring how much of each role AI can do versus what the workforce actually captures. Agent-run, end-to-end evaluation is now shipping in a major enterprise TA platform; pressure-test the interview agent for auditability and adverse impact before letting it gate candidates. — [Eightfold](https://eightfold.ai/blog/how-to-build-infinite-workforce/)

- **15 Jul 2026** — Josh Bersin cites Eightfold among multi-agent AI platforms shipping in TA, with early results showing time-to-hire dropping from two weeks to three days. — [Josh Bersin Company](https://joshbersin.com/2026/07/multi-agent-ai-for-talent-acquisition-arrives-eightfold-paradox-maki-radancy-and-more/)

## Open questions

- How does 360 Interviews handle role-specific assessment fairness when it collapses distinct evaluation dimensions into a single agent flow?
- What audit trails does Eightfold's Workforce Readiness dashboard provide for adverse-impact monitoring across job families?
